message:
type: SURVEY
files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Adding custom filters support: modifying prompt() decorator to accept optional filters param (supports both @prompt and @prompt(filters=...) usage), Prompt.from_str() and Prompt.from_file() to accept filters param, adding new create_jinja_env() helper to centralize jinja2.Environment creation + filter registration, refactoring _template_from_str() and _template_from_file() to use it.

type: SURVEY
files: outlines/prompts.py
note: Adding global filter registry: new _FILTERS dict, register_filter()/unregister_filter() functions, and modifying Prompt._template_from_str (and possibly _template_from_file) to merge global filters into env.filters alongside existing built-in filters (name, description, source, signature, schema, args).

type: DRAFT
files: outlines/prompts.py
region: create_jinja_env() helper + filter registry (_FILTERS/register_filter/unregister_filter) + Prompt.from_str/from_file/_template_from_str/_template_from_file
code: _FILTERS: Dict[str, Callable] = {}


def register_filter(name: str, fn: Callable) -> None:
    """Register a global custom Jinja2 filter available to all prompt templates."""
    _FILTERS[name] = fn


def unregister_filter(name: str) -> None:
    """Remove a previously registered global custom Jinja2 filter."""
    _FILTERS.pop(name, None)


def create_jinja_env(
    loader: Optional[jinja2.BaseLoader] = None,
    filters: Optional[Dict[str, Callable]] = None,
) -> jinja2.Environment:
    """Create the Jinja2 environment used to render prompt templates.

    Parameters
    ----------
    loader
        An optional Jinja2 loader, used when loading templates from files.
    filters
        An optional dictionary mapping custom filter names to callables. These
        are registered in addition to the built-in filters and any globally
        registered filters (see `register_filter`).

    Returns
    -------
    A `jinja2.Environment` instance with all filters registered.

    """
    env = jinja2.Environment(
        loader=loader,
        trim_blocks=True,
        lstrip_blocks=True,
        keep_trailing_newline=True,
        undefined=jinja2.StrictUndefined,
    )
    env.filters["name"] = get_fn_name
    env.filters["description"] = get_fn_description
    env.filters["source"] = get_fn_source
    env.filters["signature"] = get_fn_signature
    env.filters["schema"] = get_schema
    env.filters["args"] = get_fn_args

    env.filters.update(_FILTERS)
    if filters:
        env.filters.update(filters)

    return env


    @classmethod
    def from_str(cls, content: str, filters: Optional[Dict[str, Callable]] = None):
        """
        Create an instance of the class from a string.

        Parameters
        ----------
        content : str
            The string content to be converted into a template.
        filters : Optional[Dict[str, Callable]]
            A dictionary mapping custom filter names to callables to register
            on the Jinja2 environment used to render this template.

        Returns
        -------
        An instance of the class with the provided content as a template.
        """
        return cls(cls._template_from_str(content, filters), None)

    @classmethod
    def from_file(cls, path: Path, filters: Optional[Dict[str, Callable]] = None):
        """
        Create a Prompt instance from a file containing a Jinja template.

        Note: This method does not allow to include and inheritance to reference files
        that are outside the folder or subfolders of the file given to `from_file`.

        Parameters
        ----------
        path : Path
            The path to the file containing the Jinja template.
        filters : Optional[Dict[str, Callable]]
            A dictionary mapping custom filter names to callables to register
            on the Jinja2 environment used to render this template.

        Returns
        -------
        Prompt
            An instance of the Prompt class with the template loaded from the file.
        """
        return cls(cls._template_from_file(path, filters), None)

    @classmethod
    def _template_from_str(
        _, content: str, filters: Optional[Dict[str, Callable]] = None
    ) -> jinja2.Template:
        # Dedent, and remove extra linebreak
        cleaned_template = inspect.cleandoc(content)

        # Add linebreak if there were any extra linebreaks that
        # `cleandoc` would have removed
        ends_with_linebreak = content.replace(" ", "").endswith("\n\n")
        if ends_with_linebreak:
            cleaned_template += "\n"

        # Remove extra whitespaces, except those that immediately follow a newline symbol.
        # This is necessary to avoid introducing whitespaces after backslash `\` characters
        # used to continue to the next line without linebreak.
        cleaned_template = re.sub(r"(?![\r\n])(\b\s+)", " ", cleaned_template)

        env = create_jinja_env(filters=filters)

        return env.from_string(cleaned_template)

    @classmethod
    def _template_from_file(
        _, path: Path, filters: Optional[Dict[str, Callable]] = None
    ) -> jinja2.Template:
        file_directory = os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(path))
        env = create_jinja_env(
            loader=jinja2.FileSystemLoader(file_directory), filters=filters
        )
        return env.get_template(os.path.basename(path))
note: Merges your global filter registry with my custom create_jinja_env() helper: create_jinja_env(loader, filters) applies built-ins, then global _FILTERS, then per-call filters (per-call takes precedence). from_str/from_file/_template_from_str/_template_from_file take an optional filters param threaded through. I still need a separate prompt() decorator change (not overlapping your registry) to accept filters= and support both @prompt and @prompt(filters=...) - will draft that separately if you also touch prompt().

type: DRAFT
files: outlines/prompts.py
region: create_jinja_env() helper + global filter merge
code: _FILTERS: Dict[str, Callable] = {}


def register_filter(name: str, func: Callable) -> None:
    """Register a filter to make it available to all prompt templates.

    Parameters
    ----------
    name
        The name under which the filter will be available in templates.
    func
        The callable implementing the filter.

    """
    _FILTERS[name] = func


def unregister_filter(name: str) -> None:
    """Remove a filter from the global filter registry.

    Parameters
    ----------
    name
        The name of the filter to remove.

    """
    _FILTERS.pop(name, None)


def create_jinja_env(
    loader: Optional[jinja2.BaseLoader] = None,
    filters: Optional[Dict[str, Callable]] = None,
) -> jinja2.Environment:
    """Create the Jinja2 environment used to render prompt templates.

    Parameters
    ----------
    loader
        The Jinja2 loader to use, if any.
    filters
        Filters local to a single template. These take precedence over
        globally registered filters (see `register_filter`), which in turn
        take precedence over the built-in filters.

    Returns
    -------
    The configured Jinja2 environment.

    """
    env = jinja2.Environment(
        loader=loader,
        trim_blocks=True,
        lstrip_blocks=True,
        keep_trailing_newline=True,
        undefined=jinja2.StrictUndefined,
    )
    env.filters["name"] = get_fn_name
    env.filters["description"] = get_fn_description
    env.filters["source"] = get_fn_source
    env.filters["signature"] = get_fn_signature
    env.filters["schema"] = get_schema
    env.filters["args"] = get_fn_args
    env.filters.update(_FILTERS)
    if filters:
        env.filters.update(filters)

    return env
note: Proposing _FILTERS registry + register_filter/unregister_filter (mine, non-overlapping) live right before Prompt class; create_jinja_env (yours) takes optional filters param for local per-template filters and merges in this order: builtins -> global _FILTERS -> local filters (local wins). Please confirm this signature/order works with your from_str/from_file/prompt(filters=...) design, then I'll write it verbatim too.

note: Agreed verbatim, will write this exactly. Since create_jinja_env now auto-merges global _FILTERS internally, _template_from_str/_template_from_file don't need any change for your feature anymore - they'll just call create_jinja_env(...). I only need to add a 'filters' passthrough param to from_str/from_file/_template_from_str/_template_from_file/prompt() for local per-call filters. Sending that as a separate DRAFT next - let me know if you still need to touch those 4 methods for anything else.

code: _FILTERS: Dict[str, Callable] = {}


def register_filter(name: str, fn: Callable) -> None:
    """Register a global custom Jinja2 filter available to all prompt templates."""
    _FILTERS[name] = fn


def unregister_filter(name: str) -> None:
    """Remove a previously registered global custom Jinja2 filter."""
    _FILTERS.pop(name, None)


def create_jinja_env(
    loader: Optional[jinja2.BaseLoader] = None,
    filters: Optional[Dict[str, Callable]] = None,
) -> jinja2.Environment:
    """Create the Jinja2 environment used to render prompt templates.

    Parameters
    ----------
    loader
        An optional Jinja2 loader, used when loading templates from files.
    filters
        An optional dictionary mapping custom filter names to callables. These
        are registered in addition to the built-in filters and any globally
        registered filters (see `register_filter`).

    Returns
    -------
    A `jinja2.Environment` instance with all filters registered.

    """
    env = jinja2.Environment(
        loader=loader,
        trim_blocks=True,
        lstrip_blocks=True,
        keep_trailing_newline=True,
        undefined=jinja2.StrictUndefined,
    )
    env.filters["name"] = get_fn_name
    env.filters["description"] = get_fn_description
    env.filters["source"] = get_fn_source
    env.filters["signature"] = get_fn_signature
    env.filters["schema"] = get_schema
    env.filters["args"] = get_fn_args

    env.filters.update(_FILTERS)
    if filters:
        env.filters.update(filters)

    return env


    @classmethod
    def from_str(cls, content: str, filters: Optional[Dict[str, Callable]] = None):
        """
        Create an instance of the class from a string.

        Parameters
        ----------
        content : str
            The string content to be converted into a template.
        filters : Optional[Dict[str, Callable]]
            A dictionary mapping custom filter names to callables to register
            on the Jinja2 environment used to render this template.

        Returns
        -------
        An instance of the class with the provided content as a template.
        """
        return cls(cls._template_from_str(content, filters), None)

    @classmethod
    def from_file(cls, path: Path, filters: Optional[Dict[str, Callable]] = None):
        """
        Create a Prompt instance from a file containing a Jinja template.

        Note: This method does not allow to include and inheritance to reference files
        that are outside the folder or subfolders of the file given to `from_file`.

        Parameters
        ----------
        path : Path
            The path to the file containing the Jinja template.
        filters : Optional[Dict[str, Callable]]
            A dictionary mapping custom filter names to callables to register
            on the Jinja2 environment used to render this template.

        Returns
        -------
        Prompt
            An instance of the Prompt class with the template loaded from the file.
        """
        return cls(cls._template_from_file(path, filters), None)

    @classmethod
    def _template_from_str(
        _, content: str, filters: Optional[Dict[str, Callable]] = None
    ) -> jinja2.Template:
        # Dedent, and remove extra linebreak
        cleaned_template = inspect.cleandoc(content)

        # Add linebreak if there were any extra linebreaks that
        # `cleandoc` would have removed
        ends_with_linebreak = content.replace(" ", "").endswith("\n\n")
        if ends_with_linebreak:
            cleaned_template += "\n"

        # Remove extra whitespaces, except those that immediately follow a newline symbol.
        # This is necessary to avoid introducing whitespaces after backslash `\` characters
        # used to continue to the next line without linebreak.
        cleaned_template = re.sub(r"(?![\r\n])(\b\s+)", " ", cleaned_template)

        env = create_jinja_env(filters=filters)

        return env.from_string(cleaned_template)

    @classmethod
    def _template_from_file(
        _, path: Path, filters: Optional[Dict[str, Callable]] = None
    ) -> jinja2.Template:
        file_directory = os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(path))
        env = create_jinja_env(
            loader=jinja2.FileSystemLoader(file_directory), filters=filters
        )
        return env.get_template(os.path.basename(path))

def register_filter(name: str, func: Callable) -> None:
    """Register a filter to make it available to all prompt templates.

def create_jinja_env(
    loader: Optional[jinja2.BaseLoader] = None,
    filters: Optional[Dict[str, Callable]] = None,
) -> jinja2.Environment:
    """Create the Jinja2 environment used to render prompt templates.

    Parameters
    ----------
    loader
        The Jinja2 loader to use, if any.
    filters
        Filters local to a single template. These take precedence over
        globally registered filters (see `register_filter`), which in turn
        take precedence over the built-in filters.

        trim_blocks=True,
        lstrip_blocks=True,
        keep_trailing_newline=True,
        undefined=jinja2.StrictUndefined,
    )
    env.filters["name"] = get_fn_name
    env.filters["description"] = get_fn_description
    env.filters["source"] = get_fn_source
    env.filters["signature"] = get_fn_signature
    env.filters["schema"] = get_schema
    env.filters["args"] = get_fn_args
    env.filters.update(_FILTERS)
    if filters:
        env.filters.update(filters)

    return env
